tiles.question: T-MINUS-EVENT SIMULATION: paradigm not skill — dog-fooding snapped-between-scales logic
tiles.answer: The calibrated push system IS T-minus-event simulation in practice. Room embodies the paradigm, agent doesnt hold it in head. A T-minus PLATO room: simulation tiles track predicted event timing ± confidence. Calibration tiles record actual vs predicted, update confidence. New agents enter room, read simulation, snap into position. Paradigm shift: agent holds skill → room embodies skill. github.com/SuperInstance/plato-vessel-core/blob/main/chapters/03-oracle1-tminus-simulation-paradigm.md
